summary: Highest-value fixes focus on downscoping unsupported claims, repairing artifact lineage, and making the audited slice impossible to misread as full-benchmark evidence.

items.title: Downscope or directly test the observer-controller thesis
items.why: This is the single biggest scientific weakness. The current manuscript frames observer-versus-controller separation as a key result, but the workspace only defines that distinction and never validates it with a matched experiment.
actions: Preferred: rewrite the paper so the central contribution is bucket-level decomposition plus honest-compute lineage for one headline pair.
Alternative: add one matched observer/controller analysis that directly tests whether observer quality predicts realized gain.

items.title: Make the slice size and seed shrinkage explicit in the headline
items.why: Reviewers will react badly if `full coverage` turns out to mean 100 examples from a 1319-example benchmark and if the +3pp story is the optimistic edge of a `[+1pp, +3pp]` seed range.
actions: Add `n=100 audited slice` to the abstract, setup, and result paragraphs.
Report the seed mean/range and the reference-seed delta next to the +3pp number.
Avoid language that implies benchmark-level robustness.

items.title: Separate current audited runtime evidence from historical compute-curve context
items.why: The current runtime bundle mixes compile-true batch-57 probe facts with legacy headline rows that use different batch sizes and compile settings. That undercuts the fairness story.
actions: Either rebuild the runtime matrix under one execution regime, or split it into `historical context` and `current audited contract` sections.
Update the paper to describe exactly which runtime contract governs which claim.

items.title: Repair portability of the protocol bundle
items.why: A protocol paper loses credibility immediately if its asset lineage points to nonexistent absolute paths.
actions: Rewrite lineage fields to workspace-relative paths.
Ensure all referenced artifacts are shipped inside the current workspace or clearly marked as external dependencies.
Regenerate the manifest after the path repair.

items.title: Align locked deliverables with emitted artifacts and report the missing bucket statistics
items.why: The proposal promised canonical artifact names and richer bucket analysis than the paper currently delivers.
actions: Rename or alias `_pilot` outputs to the canonical filenames promised in the proposal.
Add per-bucket averages for `tokens_changed` and `signal value` plus a short subtype discussion of shallow versus deeper repairs.
If this remains pilot-only, say so explicitly in the proposal and manuscript.

items.title: Strengthen novelty positioning and related work
items.why: The draft still reads more like a careful internal memo than a submission-ready paper because it lacks concrete prior-art anchoring.
actions: Add citation-backed related-work anchors for DLM revision, uncertainty/calibration observers, and compute-accounting/reporting protocol work.
Replace repeated self-positioning with one crisp novelty paragraph and one explicit limitation paragraph.
